title: aquilaria brachyantha
intro: Aquilaria brachyantha, also known as the African eaglewood or stinkwood, is a species of tree in the family Thymelaeaceae. It is prized for its unique medicinal properties, which are derived from the resin that accumulates in its heartwood as a result of infection by certain fungi. The resin has been traditionally used in herbal preparations to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In traditional medicine, Aquilaria brachyantha is used in various forms, including essential oils, extracts, and infusions, to harness its health benefits and promote well-being.
